title: search --limit compatible with docker
body:
Check --limit range and update --limit manpage explanation.

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
LGTM
But it looks like you have broken the tests.  Also do we know what the default is?  Should be mentioned in the the man page.

The other part of this problem that's not solved here is if there are 200 entries in the registry and the registry returns only 100 due to the limit set by the registry itself, regardless of the limit option being used or not used by the user.  So if there are 200 entries in the registry and the user asks for all but only 100 are shown, we need to tell them at the end of the output.  Similar to what Docker does and Derrick put an example in the BZ.
And fwiw, I'm fine with merging this in the near term and then doing a separate PR for the other warning message.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *QiWang19*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 @rhatdan what was the BZ? I just hit this issue (working on another BZ) and do not understand this limit.
I don't understand the hard limit of 100 if it's up to registries to decide.
Yeah, that’s not what the bug asked for. On the contrary, if anything, the user should be able to disable the default 25 limit to get _all_ results without any limit.
